_comment: Set 068 S3 Experiment B catalogue. Pre-committed taxonomy (prereg Section 4) + the pinned surfaces (prereg Section 3a). Authored BEFORE any arm runs. snapshot_files / session_diff_files mirror build_snapshots.py's monotone layout (each file introduced once, frozen after); Q_surface_files is the end-of-set routed snippet = session-5 diff (api.py, report.py) + the headline compute file (aggregate.py), explicitly OMITTING the earlier files (quantity/registry/convert) not re-touched late. vis_at_intro is checked against session_diff_files[t0]; vis_at_close_for_Q against Q_surface_files.

defects.description: UNITS['mm'] = 0.01 but the module docstring contract says millimetre = 0.001 m (a 10x error). Visible in quantity.py alone (code contradicts its own docstring). Used downstream by convert -> aggregate -> api, so every later snapshot inherits the wrong factor. Omitted from Q's end-of-set surface.
UNITS['km'] = 100.0 but the docstring contract says kilometre = 1000.0 m. In-snippet (code vs its own docstring). Used downstream via convert; omitted from Q's surface.

build_index maps each alias to ITSELF (index[alias] = alias) instead of to the canonical unit name, contradicting the module docstring ('maps every alias to the canonical unit name'). Visible in registry.py alone. api.py (S5) normalises units through this index, so the bug surfaces only there. registry.py is omitted from Q's surface.

convert() divides value by the from-unit factor and multiplies by the to-unit factor -- the INVERSE of the documented direction ('multiply by from-factor to reach base, then divide by to-factor'). In-snippet (code vs its own docstring). aggregate + api build on convert; convert.py omitted from Q's surface.

convert() returns round(result), discarding fractional precision, contradicting its docstring ('the result is a float and MUST preserve full precision -- it never rounds'). In-snippet. Omitted from Q's surface.

mean_metres sums/divides convert() results without filtering None. convert's contract (in convert.py) says it returns None for unknown units and callers MUST drop None. Reading aggregate.py alone the code looks fine; the defect is only recognizable WITH convert.py (omitted from session-4 diff -> cross-file@intro) and convert.py is also omitted from Q's surface (cross-file@close).

convert validates units against a hardcoded local _VALID = {m,cm,mm,km} and returns None for anything else. The registry's contract (registry.py) says every alias (metre, centimetre, klick, ...) is a valid conversion input; convert silently rejects them all. Reading convert.py alone the allow-list looks self-consistent; the defect needs registry.py (omitted from session-3 diff -> cross-file@intro; also omitted from Q's surface).

sum_metres initialises the running total to 1.0 instead of 0.0 (docstring: 'starting the running total at 0.0'). Purely local to aggregate.py (in-snippet@intro). aggregate.py is in Q's surface, so end-of-set routed Q can also see it (in-snippet@close) -- isolates that R's edge must come from Q-invisibility, not mere earliness.
max_metres returns min(...) instead of max(...) (docstring: 'returns the LARGEST item'). Purely local to aggregate.py; in Q's surface (in-snippet@close).

format_total uses '%.1f' (one decimal) but the docstring contract says 3 decimal places. Local to report.py; no later snapshot builds on it (d=0), so its cost cannot grow with delay -- a built-in null for the cadence hypothesis.

total_report calls format_total(metres, label) -- arguments in the wrong order; format_total's signature is (label, metres). Visible reading api.py + report.py (both in session-5 diff -> in-snippet@intro; both in Q's surface -> in-snippet@close). d=0 (api is top-level; nothing builds on it).

Quantity.describe() returns '<unit> <value>' (unit first) but the docstring says value first ('value first then unit, e.g. 3.0 m'). In-snippet@intro. Nothing in the toolkit calls describe(), so d=0 (no-coupling). quantity.py is omitted from Q's surface (cross-file@close), but class is no-coupling regardless (d=0).
